title: asclepias lanata
intro: Asclepias lanata, also known as woolly milkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the milkweed family (Apocynaceae) and is native to North America. The plant's roots, stems, and leaves contain cardenolides, which are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The woolly milkweed is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
